extraction.one_line_summary: HAGA Chad won the 17.8km Verona ITT after beating CAMPENAERTS Victor by 4 seconds, while CARAPAZ Richard defended pink to win overall.
tactical_patterns: HAGA Chad peaked for final ITT after three weeks while CAMPENAERTS Victor lost form, suggesting Grand Tour fatigue neutralizes specialist advantage
ROGLIČ Primož gained 23 seconds on LANDA Mikel in final ITT to steal podium, showing Slovenian's superior recovery after multiweek stage racing
CARAPAZ Richard rode defensively perfect three weeks without tactical errors despite zero prior Grand Tour leadership experience, unexpected consistency

rider_signals.HAGA Chad: positive: Won 17.8km ITT at 22:07 beating time trial specialist CAMPENAERTS by 4 seconds after building form through three-week race
rider_signals.CAMPENAERTS Victor: negative: European TT champion beaten by HAGA despite being pre-stage favorite, Grand Tour fatigue clearly compromised specialist power
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Won Giro with tactically perfect execution despite zero prior Grand Tour GC experience, never put foot wrong in three weeks
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Stole final podium spot from LANDA on stage 21 ITT, gaining 23 seconds shows superior Grand Tour recovery and TT strength
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: Lost final podium place to ROGLIČ in closing ITT despite being confident pre-stage, insufficient time trial form after three weeks
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: neutral: Solid second overall after attacking repeatedly throughout race, podium return to Verona where he first stepped on Grand Tour podium
